data.body:
[G4 child-consistency re-confirm — parent 72d5 (Migrate plan-review + completion-verification onto the workflow engine) vs THIS child 5506 (B-DM: prompt + criteria library write/enumerate/index data model)]

Walked the 7 cross-child interaction modes against the parent + the whole sibling roster (0408, 101a/B4, 6b06/B2, 723c/B1, 7e48/B6, 92fa/B-UX, 961b/B-RETIRE, dc58/B3, f12d/B5). One high-confidence finding; benign readings filtered.

CLEAN modes:
- Implicit shared state: B-DM, B2, B1 all touch the prompt library, but access is non-conflicting — B2/B1 READ packaged entries; B-DM WRITES user entries to .rebar/prompts and is designed NOT to dirty the committed reviewers/index.json (test_user_write_does_not_change_committed_index). No write-write race or stale-read contradiction. CLEAN.
- Dependency gap: B-DM blocks 92fa (B-UX), which declares the dependency; B-DM lands before the UX. No missing producer/consumer link within the roster. CLEAN.
- Ordering violation: B-DM lands BEFORE B-UX, matching its own sequencing note. CLEAN.
- Consumer impact (within-roster): B-UX is the sole consumer of B-DM's enumerate/create API; B-DM left a precise hand-off (repo_root coherence, field shape). Consumer contract satisfied. CLEAN.
- Residual references: no sibling deletes/renames a symbol B-DM relies on; B6 removes only examples/code_review.yaml + templates.py, untouched by B-DM. CLEAN.

FINDING (mode: conflicting assumptions / scope overlap with the PARENT)
- finding: B-DM is structurally inconsistent with the parent epic's stated scope and its migration siblings. The parent's What/Out-of-scope/Success/Acceptance criteria are exclusively about (a) migrating plan-review onto the engine, (b) migrating completion-verification, (c) planned-trace parity, (d) cutover with signing intact, (e) removing throwaway samples — each a READ/WIRE/CUTOVER operation over the EXISTING library. The parent NEVER names a library WRITE path, an editor backend, "in-editor authoring," structured-enumerate pickers, or a create/update API. B-DM introduces exactly that (create_prompt/update_prompt, enumerate-for-pickers, validation/conflict handling, a new prompt_library.py module) — a net-new write data model whose sole declared consumer (92fa B-UX) is the visual editor, which the parent references only as a retained SAMPLE to refine against (AC5), not a deliverable. So B-DM (with B-UX) sits under this migration epic without an anchoring parent criterion, and its write-surface assumption (the library must gain a programmatic WRITE path) is not shared by the parent or any migration sibling.
- criteria: [parent What, parent Out of scope, parent Success Criteria, parent AC1, parent AC2, parent AC3, parent AC4, parent AC5, parent AC6]
- location: child 5506 Why/What/Scope/Acceptance (all five ACs); parent 72d5 What / Out of scope / Success Criteria / Acceptance Criteria
- evidence: ["Parent SC: 'plan-review and completion-verification run on the ONE workflow-engine substrate ... validated by offline planned-trace parity ...; only real workflows remain (samples removed)' — no library-write/editor-backend deliverable", "Parent What enumerates 4 bullets (migrate plan-review, migrate completion-verifier, planned-trace parity, remove sample stubs) + 'KEEP epic A's demo workflow as the unused visual-editing refinement sample' — the only visual-editing mention is RETENTION of a sample, not authoring of an editor backend", "Child 5506 ACs are entirely about a library WRITE+enumerate data model (create/update API to .rebar/prompts/<id>.md, structured-enumerate for editor pickers, validation/conflict) — none maps to any parent AC", "Child 5506 Why roots the justification in B-UX: 'The visual editor's batch-criteria work (story B-UX) must let a user SELECT criteria and prompts ... AND CREATE new ones' — an editor feature outside the parent's scope", "Whole-roster cross-check: every parent AC is covered by a migration sibling — AC1->B2(6b06)/B1(723c); AC2->B3(dc58); AC3->B4(101a); AC4->f12d+0408; AC5/AC6->B6(7e48). The migration set is complete WITHOUT B-DM, so B-DM/B-UX are additive beyond the epic's bounded deliverables.", "Sibling 92fa already carries the symmetric G3 child-without-parent-anchor finding for the editor lineage; B-DM is its backing data-model story and shares the same anchor gap."]
- scenarios: ["Epic 72d5 reaches AC6 end-state (two real workflows + one sample, lint+suite green) via B1/B2/B3/B4/B5/B6 while B-DM/B-UX are still open -> the epic's AC/SC are fully satisfiable yet two children remain, so 'epic done' is ambiguous and the acceptance set does not bound the actual deliverables.", "An implementer/reviewer tracing parent criteria -> children finds B-DM with no parent anchor and cannot tell whether the epic's migration/cleanup goal depends on the library write path or is independent of it.", "Two write paths to the prompt index now exist in this lineage (B-DM's create/update + the pre-existing prompt_authoring.save_prompt the editor already wires per B-UX's G6 review), and no parent criterion governs which is canonical — a latent drift-gate consistency risk the epic's ACs don't cover."]
- impact: Scope-broadening / traceability gap. A child carries net-new effort (a new write data-model module, validation/conflict handling, index-regen wiring, an enumerate-for-pickers superset, unit tests) that no parent acceptance/success criterion accounts for. The epic can be 'done' per AC1-AC6 while B-DM/B-UX are open (or vice-versa). No parent criterion is left UNCOVERED (the migration set is fully covered by siblings), so this is a child-without-parent-anchor / scope-misalignment consistency finding, not a coverage gap.
- checklist_item: Check the 7 cross-child modes (implicit shared state, conflicting assumptions, dependency gap, scope overlap, ordering violation, consumer impact, residual references) — each detected mode is a finding.
- suggested_fix: Either (a) add a parent acceptance criterion making the library write/enumerate data model + editor authoring an explicit epic deliverable (e.g. "the criteria/prompt library gains a write/enumerate data model so the migrated plan-review batch's criteria+prompts can be selected/authored in the visual editor, drift-gate-green"), tying B-DM + B-UX to the epic's intent and stating whether AC6 'done' is gated on it; or (b) if the library-write/editor work is a separate concern (it depends on epic A's editor, not the migration), re-parent B-DM (5506) + B-UX (92fa) out of this epic so 72d5's children are exactly the migration/parity/cleanup stories its ACs enumerate. If (a) is chosen, also note the canonical write path (B-DM create/update vs the pre-existing prompt_authoring.save_prompt) so the lineage does not keep two drift-gate write paths.

Note: mirrors the G3 structural finding already logged on sibling 92fa (B-UX); B-DM is the backing data-model half of the same un-anchored editor lineage, so resolving one (re-parent or add-parent-AC) should resolve both.
